mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of an INPUT device?
mcqs.explanation: A barcode scanner is an input device as it reads data (barcode) and inputs it into the computer system.

mcqs.question: The main function of OCR (Optical Character Recognition) software is to:

options.text: Print text documents in multiple fonts
Convert printed or handwritten text into digital format editable by computers
Enhance the resolution of images for better printing
Encrypt text files for secure transmission

mcqs.explanation: OCR technology converts scanned documents, images of text, or PDFs into machine-readable and editable text data.

mcqs.question: A scanner has a resolution of 1200 DPI. What does this specification indicate?

options.text: The scanner can process 1200 pages per minute
The scanner can capture 1200 dots per inch of the original image, determining detail capture quality
The scanner can store 1200 MB of data

mcqs.explanation: DPI (Dots Per Inch) measures the scanning resolution, indicating how many pixels the scanner can capture per linear inch, affecting image detail.

mcqs.question: Which touchscreen technology uses a layer of conductive material that senses pressure from fingers or styluses?
mcqs.explanation: Resistive touchscreens consist of two conductive layers that make contact when pressure is applied, registering the touch location.

mcqs.question: A graphic designer needs to print large architectural drawings with precise line accuracy. Which output device would be most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: Plotters are specialized output devices that draw vector graphics on large paper sizes, ideal for technical drawings and architectural plans.

mcqs.question: In Virtual Reality (VR) systems, which device is commonly used to track hand movements and gestures for interactive input?
mcqs.explanation: Data gloves equipped with motion sensors and haptic feedback are used in VR to track hand movements and provide tactile interaction.

mcqs.question: Compare three monitors: Monitor A (60Hz refresh rate, 5ms response time), Monitor B (144Hz refresh rate, 1ms response time), and Monitor C (60Hz refresh rate, 1ms response time). For fast-paced gaming, which monitor provides the BEST performance?

options.text: Monitor A because it has standard refresh rate
Monitor B because it combines high 144Hz refresh rate with low 1ms response time for smooth, blur-free motion
Monitor C because lower response time is the only factor that matters
All three monitors provide identical gaming performance

mcqs.explanation: For gaming, both high refresh rate (144Hz) and low response time (1ms) are critical—refresh rate determines how many frames display per second, while response time affects motion blur.

mcqs.question: A point-of-sale (POS) system in a retail store requires a device to quickly scan product barcodes and process sales data. The environment has high ambient light and the device must work with gloved hands. Which input device is MOST suitable for this scenario?
mcqs.explanation: Infrared barcode scanners work well in high ambient light conditions and can operate reliably with gloved hands, making them ideal for retail POS environments.
